close_reason: Created vision book with Why Turnkey, Core Principles, and Architecture Overview chapters
description:
Create a new mdbook (or section in user-manual) explaining turnkey's high-level vision and objectives.

## Key Themes to Cover

### Native Tool Compatibility
- Normal commands work as expected (go build, cargo build, npm install, etc.)
- LSP and IDE integrations work seamlessly
- Developers shouldn't need to learn new commands for basic workflows
- tw wrappers are transparent - enhance rather than replace

### Monorepo-like Environment Without Full Source Storage
- Operate like a monorepo (unified versioning, atomic changes across deps)
- But without storing source code of all dependencies in the repo
- Nix provides hermetic dependency resolution
- Buck2 cells provide the "virtual monorepo" abstraction

### Incremental Build/Test
- Reduce CI load through proper caching and incrementality
- AI agents benefit from fast feedback loops
- Only rebuild/retest what changed
- Remote execution and caching strategies

### Continuum of Experience
- From OSS-style prototype (quick iteration, minimal ceremony)
- To large-scale enterprise (governance, compliance, reproducibility)
- Same tools scale up without rewrites
- Progressive adoption - start simple, add rigor as needed

## Format
- Should be accessible to newcomers explaining "why turnkey"
- Include diagrams showing the architecture vision
- Link to detailed docs for each area
title: Create vision book documenting high-level objectives
